service: Adjudicate alleged violations of City administrative laws.
oneLiner: OATH was created in 1979 specifically to take disciplinary and administrative cases out of the hands of the same agencies bringing them — before that, agencies used their own employees as judges in cases against their own targets.
summary: Before 1979, city agencies conducted their own disciplinary and violation hearings using judges who were themselves employees of that same agency — a setup with an obvious conflict of interest. Mayor Ed Koch's Executive Order 32 in 1979 created the Office of Administrative Trials and Hearings specifically to move those cases to a centralized team of independent Administrative Law Judges separate from the agencies referring the cases. In 1988, voters made OATH a permanent charter agency, establishing it as the first municipal central tribunal of its kind in the country.

sources.label: New York City Office of Administrative Trials and Hearings — Wikipedia
